exercises.title: Simulate Path Operations with pathlib
exercises.description: Practice pathlib.Path operations using path strings (no actual disk I/O). Extract name, stem, suffix, and parent from paths. Build new paths using the / operator.
exercises.starterCode:
from pathlib import Path

# TODO: Create a Path object for '/home/alice/docs/report.txt'
# TODO: Print its name (filename with extension)
# Format: "Name: report.txt"

# TODO: Print its stem (filename without extension)
# Format: "Stem: report"

# TODO: Print its suffix (extension including dot)
# Format: "Suffix: .txt"

# TODO: Print its parent directory
# Format: "Parent: /home/alice/docs"

# TODO: Build a new path by replacing the suffix with .pdf
# Hint: use p.with_suffix('.pdf')
# Format: "PDF version: /home/alice/docs/report.pdf"

# TODO: Build a path: base = Path('data'), then join 'reports' / '2024' / 'summary.csv'
# Print: "Built: data/reports/2024/summary.csv"
exercises.expectedOutput:
Name: report.txt
Stem: report
Suffix: .txt
Parent: /home/alice/docs
PDF version: /home/alice/docs/report.pdf
Built: data/reports/2024/summary.csv
hints: p.with_suffix('.pdf') returns a new Path with changed extension

exercises.title: Simulate File Organization by Extension
exercises.description: Given a list of filenames, simulate the organize_files logic: group files by their extension and print which files go to which destination folder.
exercises.starterCode:
from pathlib import Path

files = [
    "report.txt",
    "photo.jpg",
    "data.csv",
    "notes.txt",
    "image.png",
    "script.py",
    "table.csv",
    "portrait.jpg",
]

# Extension-to-folder mapping
folders = {
    ".txt": "text",
    ".jpg": "images",
    ".png": "images",
    ".csv": "data",
    ".py": "scripts",
}

# TODO: For each file, get its suffix using Path(file).suffix
# Look up the destination folder (use 'other' if not in mapping)
# Build destination path: folder / filename
# Print: "report.txt -> text/report.txt"

# TODO: After processing all files, print the count per folder sorted alphabetically
# Format: "data: 2 files"
exercises.expectedOutput:
report.txt -> text/report.txt
photo.jpg -> images/photo.jpg
data.csv -> data/data.csv
notes.txt -> text/notes.txt
image.png -> images/image.png
script.py -> scripts/script.py
table.csv -> data/table.csv
portrait.jpg -> images/portrait.jpg
data: 2 files
images: 3 files
scripts: 1 files
text: 2 files
hints: from collections import defaultdict; counts = defaultdict(int)
for folder in sorted(counts): print(f'{folder}: {counts[folder]} files')

exercises.title: Simulate Glob Pattern Matching
exercises.description: Simulate glob pattern matching logic on a list of filenames without touching the file system. Given a virtual directory listing, filter files using pattern rules and summarize results.
exercises.starterCode:
from pathlib import Path
import fnmatch

# Virtual directory of files
virtual_files = [
    "main.py",
    "utils.py",
    "test_main.py",
    "test_utils.py",
    "data.csv",
    "config.json",
    "README.md",
    "requirements.txt",
    "__init__.py",
    "app.log",
]

# TODO: Use fnmatch.fnmatch(name, pattern) to filter
# Print all Python files (*.py)
# Format: "Python files:"
# "  main.py"

# TODO: Print all test files (test_*.py)
# Format: "Test files:"
# "  test_main.py"

# TODO: Print all non-hidden, non-dunder files
# (files that don't start with '_' and don't start with '.')
# Format: "Regular files: 8"
exercises.expectedOutput:
Python files:
  main.py
  utils.py
  test_main.py
  test_utils.py
  __init__.py
Test files:
  test_main.py
  test_utils.py
Regular files: 9
hints: py_files = [f for f in virtual_files if fnmatch.fnmatch(f, '*.py')]
test_files = [f for f in virtual_files if fnmatch.fnmatch(f, 'test_*.py')]
regular = [f for f in virtual_files if not f.startswith('_') and not f.startswith('.')]

warmup.title: Quick Recall: pathlib Path Properties
warmup.description: What does this code print? Trace each Path property.
warmup.starterCode:
from pathlib import Path

p = Path('/usr/local/bin/python3.11')
print(p.name)
print(p.stem)
print(p.suffix)
print(p.parent.name)
